title: Harmonizing Regional Standards for Salmonella Control in Food Trade
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     a     leading     cause     of     foodborne     illness     globally  ,   impacting     public     health     and     causing     significant     economic     losses   in   international     food     trade  .   Differences   in   regulatory     standards     among     countries     create     barriers  ,   delay     shipments  ,   and     increase     compliance     costs     for     exporters  .   Harmonizing   regional   standards     for     Salmonella     detection  ,   reporting  ,   and     control     can     streamline     trade     while     ensuring     high     levels     of     consumer     protection  . By   adopting     science-based  ,   risk-oriented     approaches     and     aligning     laboratory   testing   protocols  , regional   trade     blocs     can     reduce     duplication  ,   improve     surveillance     efficiency  ,   and     foster     trust     among     trading     partners  .   Such     harmonization   not   only     safeguards     public     health     but     also     strengthens     food     security  ,   boosts     competitiveness     of     agri-food     exports  ,   and     supports     sustainable     economic     growth   in   the   global   food     market  .
